title: Gothofreda capitata
taxonomy: Gothofreda capitata is classified within the family Asteraceae. This plant belongs to the order Asterales and the class Magnoliopsida. Further categorization places it in the clade Angiosperms. The genus Gothofreda is considered to be part of the tribe Inuleae. Gothofreda capitata's classification is specific to the plant's characteristics and evolutionary relationships.

intro: The plant Gothofreda capitata, also known as Asteriscus maritimus, is a species of flowering plant in the daisy family (Asteraceae) that is native to the Mediterranean region and North Africa. Gothofreda capitata has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, and its health benefits include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects, which can help to soothe digestive issues, reduce fever, and protect against cell damage. The bioactive constituents of Gothofreda capitata include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Gothofreda capitata include teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are used to treat a range of health conditions, including digestive issues, fever, and skin problems.
